human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for broadcast, film, and streaming production specificity, source quality, permit and signatory evidence handling, safety and content-security boundary discipline, and validated delivery completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to production-compliance work grounded in permits, certificates of insurance, guild and union workpapers, call-sheet safety attachments, and content-security evidence.
Authority sources cover film-office operating guidance, SAG-AFTRA and DGA workflow references, OSHA and CSATF safety controls, and TPN content-security standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing permit or signatory evidence, safety and schedule conflicts, legal-signoff refusal, rights and broadcaster-boundary rejection, content-security gaps, low-confidence intake, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package remains narrow enough to avoid generic functional compliance behavior and repeatedly routes legal, broadcaster, payroll, rights-clearance, safety-signoff, and security-certification work to adjacent owners.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around permit approval, union interpretation, broadcaster standards, rights clearance, safety signoff, and vendor-security certification.

notes: The package is suitable as a validated broadcast, film, and streaming production compliance specialist for spec-pack distribution and JIT materialization under orchestrator control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
